lanes.name: Lane A — Physiology-rich sleep and circadian programs

targets.name: Brigham and Women's Hospital — Division of Sleep and Circadian Disorders
targets.ask: participant-night sleep data with repeated stress or symptom burden plus physiology-rich subset

targets.name: Stanford Medicine — Center for Sleep and Circadian Sciences
targets.ask: nightly wearable datasets with repeated next-day burden or behavioral outcomes

targets.name: Northwestern University / Northwestern Medicine — CSCB + Center for Circadian & Sleep Medicine
targets.ask: participant-night sleep datasets with repeated burden outcomes and linked clinical or wearable phenotyping

targets.name: Hospital Clínic Barcelona — Unit of Sleep Research
targets.ask: longitudinal sleep disorder cohort with participant-night rows, repeated burden outcomes, and biomarker or PSG subset

lanes.name: Lane B — Digital trials and wearable-first cohorts

targets.name: Scripps Research — Digital Trials Center
targets.ask: participant-night exports with sleep, HR, activity, and repeated burden endpoints

targets.name: Duke University Population Research Institute — RAISE / mobile stress study line
targets.ask: repeated stress-linked wearable cohorts preserving participant-day or participant-night linkage

lanes.name: Lane C — Platform and device partners for prospective pilots

targets.name: Oura for Research
targets.ask: prospective pilot or access path through an academic collaborator using Oura data with repeated burden reporting

targets.name: Empatica — Health Monitoring Platform / Research Portal
targets.ask: prospective study with autonomic, movement, and sleep-relevant physiology and raw data access

targets.name: Withings Health Solutions
targets.ask: prospective pilot combining nightly sleep summaries with repeated burden outcomes and optional home sleep hardware
